## Structure

UDP S→C 0x03/0x07/0x02 — **Multipart/CharsysInfo**. Multipart
chain with discriminator `0x02` (CharsysInfo per
`multipart_framing.md`). Reassembled bodies range 1878-4351
bytes across 3 logical packets / 12 fragments.

See `multipart_framing.md` for the canonical fragment-header
layout:

```
Per-fragment header (6B):
  [0x00]                 chain_key (always 0x00 for retail)
  [discriminator=0x02]   CharsysInfo identifier
  [total_size LE32]      total reassembled body size
[fragment payload]
```

Sample reassembled body openings (first 32 bytes):
```
00 00 05 00 00 00 02 ff 10 00 00 22 02 01 01 0a 00 fa 0e ff 9d fe 85 01 00 11 00 02 1c 00 04 04
   discriminator-2 carries CharsysInfo-style block payload
```

Per `charsys_dead_code.md`: case 0xb3 in FULLCHARSYSTEM
dispatcher is **dead code** in NC2 Evolution. The
CharsysInfo discriminator may persist for legacy reasons but
serves no live purpose in the modern client.

## Variants

3 reassembled logical packets / 12 fragments. Fragment sizes
312-1011 bytes (matches the 1011B max we saw in `udp_c2s_07`
unreliable multipart).

## Observed contexts

Concentrated in VEHICLE_DRONE (×2) and CREATION_LEVELING_LONG
(×1). The 15 captures that don't see this don't enter the
flows that emit CharsysInfo multipart bursts.

## Open questions

- Per `charsys_dead_code.md` — discriminator 0x02 is largely
  dead code. Why does the modern server still emit these?
  Possibly for backward-compat with older client builds.
- The 4351B max fragmented body suggests the actual content is
  the full character / faction / clan state — but the client
  appears to ignore it.

## Server-side handler

Multipart reassembly handled by
`server.gameserver.packets.GamePacketReaderUDP` per
`multipart_framing.md`. Discriminator 0x02 routes to a
no-op consumer (per dead-code memory).

For full retail parity: emit dummy CharsysInfo multipart
chains with arbitrary content — modern client ignores the
body but expects the channel to be active.
